assistant_summary: {"findings":[{"title":"[P1] Base staleness checks on the last refresh point","body":"`check_staleness()` always uses `decision.created_at` as the `git log --since` boundary. In common workflows where a user marks a decision fresh after reviewing it, or links a file long after the decision was created, older commits are still in range, so the same file is reported as stale again immediately even though nothing changed since the last review/link. That makes the new `decision stale --status fresh` / `stale-all` flow unable to persist a “reviewed and current” state.","confidence_score":0.97,"priority":1,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/core/decisions.py","line_range":{"start":557,"end":562}}},{"title":"[P2] Scan every fresh decision in `stale-all`","body":"`decision stale-all` is advertised as checking all fresh decisions, but it hard-codes `limit=1000` when loading them. On repos that accumulate more than 1000 fresh decisions, the tail of the dataset is silently skipped and can never be auto-marked stale by this command, which is a correctness issue rather than just a pagination concern because the CLI does not expose any way to continue from the 1000th row.","confidence_score":0.89,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/cli/decisions_cmds.py","line_range":{"start":346,"end":346}}}],"overall_correctness":"patch is incorrect","overall_explanation":"The new staleness workflow has a logic bug that makes refreshed decisions immediately appear stale again, and the bulk checker silently ignores decisions beyond the first 1000 rows. Those issues mean the patch changes behavior incorrectly in realistic repositories.","overall_confidence_score":0.94}
